studyName: CIP: Differential Response to Hydroxyurea and Incidence of Stroke in Sickle Cell Disease

tables.description: This subject phenotype table includes subject gender, age that hydroxyurea was initiated, maximum tolerated hydroxyurea dose, baseline fetal hemoglobin level, fetal hemoglobin level at the maximum tolerated dose (MTD) of hydroxyurea, and delta of fetal hemoglobin levels between baseline and MTD.

variables.name: BL F/F+S
variables.description: Baseline fetal hemoglobin (HbF) before starting hydroxyurea (%HbF/%HbF+ %HbS)*100

variables.name: DELTA HbF
variables.description: (MTD F/F+S)-(BL F/F+S), where MTD F/F+S = Fetal hemoglobin (HbF) at the maximum hydroxyurea dose and BL F/F+S = Baseline fetal hemoglobin (HbF) before starting hydroxyurea (%HbF/%HbF+ %HbS)*100.

variables.name: HU mg/kg
variables.description: Maximum tolerated daily dose of hydroxyurea in milligrams per kilogram

variables.name: MTD F/F+S
variables.description: Fetal hemoglobin (HbF) at the maximum hydroxyurea dose

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Gender of participant

variables.name: START_AGE
variables.description: Age they initiated the drug, hydroxyurea, in years

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

variables.name: BL F/F+S
variables.description: Baseline fetal hemoglobin (HbF) before starting hydroxyurea (%HbF/%HbF+ %HbS)*100

variables.name: DELTA HbF
variables.description: (MTD F/F+S)-(BL F/F+S), where MTD F/F+S = Fetal hemoglobin (HbF) at the maximum hydroxyurea dose and BL F/F+S = Baseline fetal hemoglobin (HbF) before starting hydroxyurea (%HbF/%HbF+ %HbS)*100.

variables.name: HU mg/kg
variables.description: Maximum tolerated daily dose of hydroxyurea in milligrams per kilogram

variables.name: MTD F/F+S
variables.description: Fetal hemoglobin (HbF) at the maximum hydroxyurea dose

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Gender of participant

variables.name: START_AGE
variables.description: Age they initiated the drug, hydroxyurea, in years

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

tables.description: This data table contains subject IDs, consent information, affection status, and subject aliases.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJID
variables.description: Subject ID used in the Source Repository

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SUBJ_SOURCE
variables.description: Source repository where subjects originate

tables.description: This data table contains mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs. The data table also includes sample aliases and sample use.

variables.name: SAMPID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SAMPLE_USE
variables.description: Sample Use

variables.name: SAMP_SOURCE
variables.description: Source repository where samples originate. HUSTLE=Long Term Effects of Hydroxyurea Therapy in Children with Sickle Cell Disease; SWITCH=Stroke with Transfusions Changing to Hydroxyurea

variables.name: SOURCE_SAMPID
variables.description: Sample ID used in the Source Repository. HUSTLE=Long Term Effects of Hydroxyurea Therapy in Children with Sickle Cell Disease; SWITCH=Stroke with Transfusions Changing to Hydroxyurea

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: This sample attributes table includes body site where sample was extracted and analyte type.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte Type

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: SAMPID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID
